6a7fe245.name: Nikon Nikkorex
6a7fe245.description: The Nikkorex is a 35mm camera made by Nikon.

116949d6.name: Nikon F-601
116949d6.description: The Nikon F-601 is a 35mm camera introduced by Nikon in 1990. It features Electronically controlled vertical-travel focal plane shutter, 5 zone matrix or centre-weighted metering metering. It weighs 565 g.

1f97bb58.name: Argus C3
1f97bb58.description: The Argus C3 was a low-priced rangefinder camera mass-produced from 1939 to 1966 by Argus in Ann Arbor, Michigan, United States. The camera sold over 2.2 million units, making it one of the most popular American cameras in history.

f405c410.name: Agfa Optima 1535 Sensor
f405c410.description: The Agfa Optima 1535 Sensor is a 35mm camera made by Agfa. It features Agfa Solitar S f2.8/40, 4 elements in 3 groups lens mount.

specs.lens: Agfa Solitar S f2.8/40, 4 elements in 3 groups

e4989181.name: Canon P
e4989181.description: The Canon P (P for Populaire) was a rangefinder camera produced by Canon Inc., compatible with the Leica M39 screw mount (LTM). It was introduced in March 1959, alongside an all-black version, and was marketed as a low-cost sister to the Canon VI-L.

6cf28893.name: Canon VT
6cf28893.description: The Canon VT is a rangefinder camera released by Canon in 1956. Until then, Canon had a history of making slightly modified Leica copies.

0dc0a56e.name: Yashica Electro 35
0dc0a56e.description: The Yashica Electro 35 is a 35mm camera introduced by Yashica in 1966.

7fd48349.name: Mamiya C
7fd48349.description: The Mamiya C is a medium format (120) medium format camera produced by Mamiya from 1956 to 1994. It features Mamiya C lens mount, in lens (leaf) shutter, in optional viewfinders or external metering.

e3c2e1e8.name: Nikon S3
e3c2e1e8.description: The Nikon S3 is a 35mm camera produced by Nikon from 1958 to 1954. It features Nikon 'S' bayonet mount lens mount, mechanically timed shutter, no integrated meter metering. It weighs 590 g.

ebad9ed2.name: Nikon SP
ebad9ed2.description: The Nikon SP is a 35mm camera produced by Nikon from 1957 to 1964. It features Nikon 'S' bayonet mount lens mount, mechanically timed shutter, no integrated meter metering.

f6488607.name: Kodak Super Kodak 620
f6488607.description: The Super Kodak 620 is a 35mm camera made by Kodak.

a4b50a87.name: Fujifilm Instax Mini 9
a4b50a87.description: The Instax Mini 9 is a Instant camera introduced by Fujifilm in 2017.

9b8b00d3.name: Optique & Précision de Levallois OPL Foca
9b8b00d3.description: The OPL Foca is a camera introduced by Optique & Précision de Levallois in 1945.

d91be0cd.name: Agfa Click
d91be0cd.description: The Agfa Click is a 35mm camera made by Agfa.

c18259c5.name: Concord Le Clic LC17
c18259c5.description: The Concord Le Clic LC17 is a camera made by Concord.

805294ee.name: Canon camera
805294ee.description: The Canon camera is a 35mm camera made by Canon.

c55280cf.name: Polaroid 35mm AF
c55280cf.description: The Polaroid 35mm AF is a Instant camera introduced by Polaroid in 1994.

6ce524c6.name: Société Lumière Lumière Super ELJY
6ce524c6.description: The Lumière Super ELJY is a camera introduced by Société Lumière in 1939.

323fb408.name: Minolta
323fb408.description: The Minolta is a 35mm SLR camera produced by Minolta from 1977 to 1984. It features Minolta SR mount lens mount.

a3fa5575.name: Olympus XZ-1
a3fa5575.description: The Olympus XZ-1 is a 35mm camera introduced by Olympus in 1979.

7a963051.name: Agfa Optima 1a
7a963051.description: First produced in 1962, the Agfa Optima 1a or Agfamatic was one of the first fully automatic scale-focusing 35mm film cameras.

535ba086.name: Agfa Ambiflex
535ba086.description: The Agfa Ambiflex was a SLR for 35mm film, made by Agfa in c.1960. It was made for interchangeable lenses but had a leaf shutter.

11bc4522.name: Canon AF35M
11bc4522.description: The Canon AF35M (also known as the Sure Shot in North America and the Autoboy in Japan) is a 35mm point-and-shoot compact camera by Canon Inc.. Introduced in November 1979, it was Canon's first compact camera with an autofocus feature.

b3f8f93c.name: Canon Epoca
b3f8f93c.description: The Canon Epoca (also known as the Photura in North America and the Autoboy Jet in Japan) is a 35mm bridge camera by Canon Inc.

fa298fe5.name: Canon 7
fa298fe5.description: The Canon 7 is a focal-plane shutter rangefinder system camera with an integrated selenium light meter  introduced by Canon Inc. in September 1961, the last model compatible with the Leica M39 lens mount.

specs.lens: Leica M39 Screw Mount with special bayonet mount for 50 mm f0.95 lens and the Canon Mirror Housing 2

de0cd950.name: Canon II
de0cd950.description: The Canon rangefinders of the late 1940s and early 1950s are Leica-compatible screw-mount cameras. Many were brought to the U.S.

5838d560.name: Canonet G-III QL17
5838d560.description: The Canon Canonet G-III QL17 is a coupled-rangefinder, leaf-shuttered, fixed-focal-length 35 mm camera first manufactured by Canon between 1972 and 1982. It features fully shutter-priority automatic exposure and fully manual shooting modes.

30543aff.name: Canon EF-M camera
30543aff.description: The Canon EF-M was a manual-focus 35mm film, SLR camera which used the Canon EF lens mount. It was introduced in 1991 for export to the non-Japanese market, and was the only manual focus camera in the EF line.

147e5a3c.name: Contax I
147e5a3c.description: The Contax I, or Original Contax, is a 35 mm rangefinder camera made between 1932 and 1936 by Zeiss Ikon. The Contax I had six identifiable variants, but fundamentally identical; every aspect was designed to outperform the Leica.

3e951311.name: Contax G
3e951311.description: The Contax G camera line consists of two cameras, the G1 and G2, interchangeable-lens cameras sold by Kyocera under the Contax brand in competition with the Leica M7, Cosina Voigtländer Bessa-R, and Konica Hexar RF.

2e3ecda4.name: Fujifilm Instax Wide 300
2e3ecda4.description: The Fujifilm Instax Wide 300 is an analog instant camera from Fujifilm that uses Instax Wide instant film. It has a moderately wide-angle lens with some manually selectable fixed focus zones and a fixed aperture.

7da1bbcf.name: Instax Mini 11
7da1bbcf.description: The Instax Mini 11 is an analog instant film camera designed, developed, marketed and sold by Fujifilm. The Instax camera is the successor to the Instax Mini 9 and was released on March 5, 2020.

55106ac7.name: Fujifilm Fuji GX680
55106ac7.description: The Fuji GX680 is a series of single lens reflex system cameras for medium format film produced by Fujifilm with interchangeable camera lenses and interchangeable film holders for the unusual film format 6×8 cm on 120 and 220 roll film.

80175358.name: Kodak 35
80175358.description: The Kodak 35 was introduced in 1938 as the first US manufactured 35 mm camera from Eastman Kodak Company. It was developed in Rochester, New York, when it became likely that imports from the Kodak AG factory in Germany could be disrupted by war.

ac994490.name: Kodak Brownie
ac994490.description: The Brownie was a series of camera models made by Eastman Kodak and first released in 1900.

c0ed3cae.name: Kodak Stereo Camera
c0ed3cae.description: The Kodak Stereo Camera was a 35mm film stereo camera produced between 1954 and 1959. Similar to the Stereo Realist, the camera employed two lenses to take twin shots of scenes, which could then be viewed in dedicated image viewers. The lenses supported adjustable apertures and variable shutter speeds.

4e1f9b3d.name: Kodak 35 Rangefinder
4e1f9b3d.description: The Kodak 35 Rangefinder is an improved version of the Kodak 35 that  was launched by the Eastman Kodak Company in 1938 as their first 35mm camera manufactured in the USA.

701d8af5.name: Kodak Retina Reflex
701d8af5.description: The Kodak Retina Reflex is a discontinued series of four single-lens reflex cameras made by Kodak in Germany between 1957 and 1974, as part of the Kodak Retina line of 35mm film cameras.

specs.lens: model specific: Retina convertible or DKL-mount lens bayonet

c7542a3e.name: Konica Hexar
c7542a3e.description:
The Konica Hexar is a 35 mm fixed-lens,
fixed focal length autofocus camera which was produced through the 1990s. It was introduced to the market in 1993. While styled like a rangefinder camera, and intended for a similar style of photography, in specification it is more like a larger "point and shoot" camera.

fefabada.name: Konica FC-1
fefabada.description: The Konica FC-1 was introduced in 1980 by Konica, one year after the FS-1, but does not have the integrated winder. It was manufactured until 1983. Both models were only available in a black finish.

0b8d0d09.name: Leica minilux
0b8d0d09.description: The Leica minilux is the first in a series of four luxury titanium-clad point and shoot cameras that were produced by Leica Camera starting from 1995; it is equipped with a high-quality lens and body to compete with similar premium compact cameras produced during the Japanese bubble-economy era, including the Contax T line, Konica Hexar, Nikon 28Ti/35Ti, Minolta TC-1, Ricoh GR series, and Rollei QZ 35W/35T.

f65b628f.name: Leica CL
f65b628f.description: The Leica CL is a 35mm compact rangefinder camera with interchangeable lenses in the Leica M-mount. It was developed in collaboration with Minolta who manufactured it. It first appeared in April 1973 and was released in the Japanese market in November 1973 as the Leitz Minolta CL.

a954f221.name: Leica II
a954f221.description: The Leica II is a Barnack rangefinder camera introduced by Leica in 1932. They were the first Leica cameras with a built-in rangefinder. Several models were produced over the years, in parallel with the Leica III series from 1933.

0b75bb0c.name: Leica M-A
0b75bb0c.description: The Leica M-A (Typ 127) is a purely mechanical 35 mm rangefinder camera released by Leica Camera AG in 2014. The camera has no exposure meter, no electronic control, and no battery is required to operate it. The camera is Leica's first purely mechanical camera since the release of the Leica M4-P in 1981.

specs.shutter: mechanically timed horizontal running cloth shutter

d6e6fe3f.name: Leica M1
d6e6fe3f.description: The Leica M1 is a 35 mm camera by Leica Camera AG, introduced in 1959. The M1 has a parallax-corrected viewfinder with frames for 35 and 50mm permanently displayed. It was designed for scientific and military use.

specs.shutter: Focal plane, metal curtains, vertical travel

53a980b0.name: Leica M2
53a980b0.description: The Leica M2 is a 35 mm rangefinder camera by Ernst Leitz GmbH of Wetzlar, Germany,  introduced in 1957. Around 82,000 M2s were produced between 1957 and 1968. Around 1500 M2s were produced by Ernst Leitz Canada, but most of these are not marked as such on the top plate.

9b9f4528.name: Leica M3
9b9f4528.description: The Leica M3 is a 35 mm rangefinder camera by Ernst Leitz GmbH (now Leica Camera AG), introduced in 1954. It was a new starting point for Leitz, which until then had only produced screw-mount Leica cameras that were incremental improvements to its original Leica (Ur-Leica).

a3113c74.name: Leica M4
a3113c74.description: The Leica M4 is a 35mm 35 mm camera made by Leica. It features Leica M-mount lens mount. It weighs 560 g.

2dcb5cec.name: Leica M5
2dcb5cec.description: The Leica M5 is a 35 mm camera by Leica Camera AG, introduced in 1971. It was the first Leica rangefinder camera to feature through-the-lens (TTL) metering and the last to be made entirely in Wetzlar by hand using the traditional "adjust and fit" method.

specs.shutter: Mechanically timed horizontal running cloth shutter

7ef7dde1.name: Leica M6
7ef7dde1.description: The Leica M6 is a 35mm rangefinder camera made by Leica. It features Leica M-mount lens mount, Mechanically timed horizontal running cloth shutter shutter.

specs.shutter: Mechanically timed horizontal running cloth shutter

3476b192.name: Leica MP
3476b192.description: The Leica MP is a 35mm camera made by Leica. It features mechanically timed horizontal running cloth shutter shutter, 12 mm Silicon Photodiode metering.

specs.shutter: mechanically timed horizontal running cloth shutter

e8705b09.name: Leica 250 Reporter
e8705b09.description: The Leica 250 Reporter is a 35mm rangefinder camera made by Leica. It features Leica screw mount lens mount.

b63a974d.name: Leica Standard
b63a974d.description: The Leica Standard, Model E was the fourth version of the original 35 mm Leica camera to be launched from Ernst Leitz in Wetzlar, Germany. The concept was conceived by their employee Oskar Barnack in 1913.

9b1e285e.name: Mamiya Press
9b1e285e.description: The Mamiya Press is a 35mm rangefinder camera introduced by Mamiya in 1963. It features Mamiya Press bayonet mount lens mount, leaf shutter in lens shutter.

d10f03ef.name: Mamiya 645
d10f03ef.description: The Mamiya 645 is a medium format (120) medium format camera introduced by Mamiya in 1975.

1a8bfc44.name: Mamiya RB67
1a8bfc44.description: The Mamiya RB67 is a 35mm SLR camera produced by Mamiya from 1970 to 2010. It features Custom Mamiya breech-lock bayonet mount lens mount.

5828633b.name: Minolta 35
5828633b.description: The Minolta-35 was launched in the spring of 1947 by Chiyoda Kogaku. It was the first successful new 35mm rangefinder camera with Leica specifications to emerge on the market after World War II that uses the 39mm screw lens-mount.

1a0cd862.name: Minolta Hi-Matic
1a0cd862.description: Hi-Matic was the name of a long-running series of 35 mm cameras made by Minolta.

95c7a2a0.name: Minolta Maxxum 9000
95c7a2a0.description: The Minolta 9000 AF is a professional Single-lens reflex autofocus camera, introduced by Minolta in August 1985. It was both Minolta's and the world's first professional autofocus SLR.

a27e17c6.name: Minolta TC-1
a27e17c6.description: TC-1 is a luxury point and shoot camera that was produced by Minolta. It is a compact 35 mm clad in titanium, equipped with a G-Rokkor 28mm f/3.5 lens.

c3fbdc6a.name: Minolta AL-F
c3fbdc6a.description: The Minolta AL-F was a rangefinder camera launched by Minolta in 1967. The AL-F was an automatic 35 mm camera.

bd35481a.name: Minolta 110 Zoom SLR
bd35481a.description: The Minolta 110 Zoom SLR is a 35mm SLR camera introduced by Minolta in 1976. It features Fixed 25-50 mm zoom lens lens mount.

46e6837d.name: Minolta XG-1
46e6837d.description: The Minolta XG-1 is a 35mm SLR camera introduced by Minolta in 1979. It features Minolta SR-mount lens mount, Aperture priority, manual shutter, Full aperture TTL, center-weighted metering. It weighs 490 g.

245289a9.name: Nikon Ti cameras
245289a9.description: The Nikon 35Ti (1993) and Nikon 28Ti (1994) are luxury titanium-clad point and shoot cameras that were produced by Nikon, equipped with a high-quality lens and body, competing with similar luxury compact cameras produced during the Japanese bubble-economy era, including the Contax T line, Konica Hexar, Leica minilux, Minolta TC-1, Ricoh GR series, and Rollei QZ 35W/35T.

3e10996c.name: Nikon EM
3e10996c.description: The Nikon EM is a beginner's level, interchangeable lens, 35 mm film, single lens reflex (SLR) camera. It was manufactured by Nippon Kogaku K.

82db91a5.name: Nikon F2
82db91a5.description: The Nikon F2 is a professional-level 35 mm single-lens reflex (SLR) camera. It was manufactured by the Japanese optics company Nippon Kogaku K.

specs.metering: TTL metering, EV 1 to EV 18 at ASA/ISO 100 and with 50mm f/1.4 lens

fb1da1df.name: Nikon F3
fb1da1df.description: The Nikon F3 is Nikon's third professional single-lens reflex camera body, preceded by the F and F2.

specs.metering: silicon photodiode light meter, TTL metering, EV 1 to EV 18 (f/1.4 at 1s to f/16 at 1/1000s at ASA/ISO 100 and with 50 mm f/1.4 lens

71a50be8.name: Nikon FA
71a50be8.description: The Nikon FA is an advanced amateur-level, interchangeable lens, 35 mm film, single-lens reflex (SLR) camera. It was manufactured by the Japanese optics company Nippon Kogaku K.

7cf85a6f.name: Nikon FE
7cf85a6f.description: The Nikon FE is an advanced semi-professional level, interchangeable lens, 35 mm film, single-lens reflex (SLR) camera. It was manufactured by Nikon in Japan from 1978 to 1983, and was available new from dealer stock until c.

81b364aa.name: Nikon FG
81b364aa.description: The Nikon FG is an interchangeable lens, 35 mm film, single-lens reflex (SLR) camera. It was manufactured by Nippon Kogaku K.

specs.shutter: electronically controlled, double speed mechanical backup
specs.metering: silicon photodiode light meter, TTL metering, EV 1 to EV 18 (f/1.4 at 1s to f/16 at 1/1000s at ASA/ISO 100 and with 50mm f/1.4 lens

bc1a298e.name: Nikon FG-20
bc1a298e.description: The Nikon FG-20 is a 35 mm film single-lens reflex (SLR) camera with interchangeable lenses. It was released in 1984 by Nippon Kogaku K.

specs.metering: TTL metering, EV 1 to EV 18 at ASA/ISO 100 and with 50 mm f/1.4 lens

863c4657.name: Nikon FM
863c4657.description: The Nikon FM is a mechanically operated, interchangeable lens, 35 mm film, single-lens reflex (SLR) camera. It was made in Japan between 1977 and 1982 by Nippon Kogaku K.

specs.metering: TTL with gallium arsenide phosphide photodiode

9029039a.name: Nikon FM3A
9029039a.description: The Nikon FM3A is an interchangeable-lens, focal-plane shutter, 35 mm film, single-lens reflex (SLR) camera. It was manufactured by Nikon Corporation in Japan on small-volume assembly lines, from 2001 to 2006.

specs.metering: silicon photodiode light meter, TTL full aperture metering, EV 1 to EV 20 (f/1.4 at 1s to f/16 at 1/1000s at ASA/ISO 100 and with 50 mm f/1.4 lens

69bf6a42.name: Nikon FM10
69bf6a42.description: The Nikon FM10 is a manual focus 35 mm film camera formerly sold by Nikon Corporation. It is of SLR design and was first available in 1995.

specs.metering: silicon photodiode light meter, TTL metering

daab65ed.name: Olympus Stylus Epic
daab65ed.description: The Olympus Stylus Epic is a 35mm compact camera made by Olympus. It features Fixed lens mount. It weighs 145 g.

489885eb.name: Olympus Trip 35
489885eb.description: The Olympus Trip 35 is a 35mm compact camera made by Olympus. It features fixed lens lens mount.

5258cbdc.name: Olympus 35RC
5258cbdc.description: The Olympus 35RC is a 35mm rangefinder camera introduced by Olympus in 1970. It features fixed lens lens mount.

0f363955.name: Olympus 35SP
0f363955.description: The Olympus 35SP is a 35mm camera produced by Olympus from 1955 to 1958. It features fixed lens lens mount.

54726c3a.name: Olympus OM-1
54726c3a.description: The Olympus OM-1 is a manually operated 35mm single-lens reflex (SLR) professional camera, part of the Olympus OM system. It was manufactured by Olympus Optical Co., Ltd., later Olympus Corporation, in Japan between 1972 and 1988.

5ded6a79.name: Olympus Pen F
5ded6a79.description: The Olympus Pen F is a 35mm SLR camera produced by Olympus from 1963 to 1972. It features Olympus Pen F mount lens mount.

a52ec620.name: Pentax K-01
a52ec620.description: The Pentax K-01 is a 35mm camera made by Pentax. It features Interchangeable Pentax KAF3 and KAF2 mount compatible with Pentax auto-aperture lenses; older lenses supported in stop-down metering mode lens mount, EV -1 to 21 (ISO 100, 50mm 1.4) metering. It weighs 480 g.

specs.lens: Interchangeable Pentax KAF3 and KAF2 mount compatible with Pentax auto-aperture lenses; older lenses supported in stop-down metering mode

73444eb0.name: Pentax K1000
73444eb0.description: The Pentax K1000 is a 35mm SLR camera produced by Pentax from 1976 to 1997. It features Pentax K bayonet mount lens mount.

cfc84153.name: Pentax LX
cfc84153.description: The Pentax LX is a 35mm SLR camera produced by Pentax from 1980 to 2001. It features Pentax K lens mount, Horizontal-running titanium shutter, mechanical or electr... shutter, Off-the-film (OTF) TTL metering.

specs.shutter: Horizontal-running titanium shutter, mechanical or electronic operation

01e00bc8.name: Pentax ME
01e00bc8.description: The Pentax ME is a 35mm SLR camera produced by Pentax from 1977 to 1979. It features K mount lens mount, 1 – 1/1000 s, Bulb shutter.

a77ee2c5.name: Pentax ME Super
a77ee2c5.description: The Pentax ME Super is a 35mm SLR camera produced by Pentax from 1979 to 1984. It features Pentax K-F mount lens mount, 4 – 1/2000 s shutter.

6acb346b.name: Pentax MG
6acb346b.description: The Pentax MG is a 35mm SLR camera produced by Pentax from 1981 to 1985. It features Pentax K bayonet mount lens mount.

0721c07d.name: Pentax MX
0721c07d.description: The Pentax MX is a 35mm SLR camera produced by Pentax from 1976 to 1985. It features K mount lens mount, 1 – 1/1000 s, Bulb shutter.

a3789e60.name: Pentax MZ-S
a3789e60.description:
The Pentax MZ-S is a 35mm single-lens reflex camera from Pentax of Japan. It was introduced in 2001 
and discontinued in February 2006. It is closely related to the prototype MZ-D Full-frame digital SLR, which never entered production.

fbeef48b.name: Pentax Q10
fbeef48b.description: The Pentax Q10 is a 35mm camera made by Pentax. It features Pentax Q lens mount, 1/2000s to 30s leaf, 1/8000s to 2s electronic shutter. It weighs 200 g.

15344c15.name: Pentax 6×7
15344c15.description: The Pentax 6×7 is a 35mm SLR camera introduced by Pentax in 1989.

155ce107.name: Pentax 645
155ce107.description: The Pentax 645 is a 35mm SLR camera introduced by Pentax in 1984. It features Pentax 645 A mount (70.87 mm) lens mount, vertical cloth focal plane shutter, TTL center-weighted metering.

f54816f9.name: Pentax Spotmatic
f54816f9.description: The Pentax Spotmatic is a 35mm SLR camera produced by Pentax from 1964 to 1976. It features M42 screw mount lens mount, Mechanical shutter, Average, through the lens (TTL) metering.

7a0da13e.name: Polaroid Impulse
7a0da13e.description: The Polaroid Impulse is a Instant 3-element 116mm f/9.4 plastic lens produced by Polaroid from 1988 to 1994.

fec1ae17.name: Polaroid 20×24 camera
fec1ae17.description: The Polaroid 20×24 camera is a 4×5 large format View camera introduced by Polaroid in 1976. It features Between-lens shutter, External meter required metering.

a13f5d69.name: Polaroid SX-70
a13f5d69.description: The Polaroid SX-70 is a 35mm SLR camera produced by Polaroid from 1972 to 1981.

9d446bb8.name: Polaroid Spice Cam
9d446bb8.description: The Spice Cam is a Instant Instant introduced by Polaroid in 1997.

7cab9863.name: Ricoh XR-P
7cab9863.description: The Ricoh XR-P is a 35mm SLR camera introduced by Ricoh in 1984. It features Ricoh System RK mount lens mount.

09020e66.name: Rolleiflex 6000 System
09020e66.description: The Rolleiflex 6000 System is a 35mm SLR camera made by Rollei.

daec4d5a.name: Rolleiflex Hy6
daec4d5a.description: The Rolleiflex Hy6 is a 35mm SLR camera made by Rollei.

e96ca5d0.name: Rolleiflex SL35
e96ca5d0.description: The Rolleiflex SL35 is a 35mm SLR camera produced by Rollei from 1970 to 1982. It features QBM (Quick Bayonet Mount) lens mount.

3e733317.name: Rolleiflex SL66
3e733317.description: The Rolleiflex SL66 is a 35mm SLR camera produced by Rollei from 1968 to 1973.

04a91c5f.name: Rolleiflex SL2000F
04a91c5f.description: The Rolleiflex SL2000F is a 35mm SLR camera produced by Rollei from 1980 to 1984. It features QBM lens mount, Electronically timed vertical running metal shutter shutter.

specs.shutter: Electronically timed vertical running metal shutter

0339ab39.name: Samsung Electronics Samsung Galaxy NX
0339ab39.description: The Samsung Galaxy NX is a 35mm SLR camera made by Samsung Electronics. It features Electronically controlled vertical-run focal plane shutter shutter, 221-segment TTL metering. It weighs 495 g.

specs.shutter: Electronically controlled vertical-run focal plane shutter

076b3052.name: Sony FX6
076b3052.description: The Sony FX6 (ILME-FX6) is a full-frame mirrorless, interchangeable-lens, semi-professional cinema camera announced on 17 November 2020. The model is positioned between the smaller Sony ILME-FX3 and larger Sony PXW-FX9.

289ca1de.name: Voigtlander Voigtländer Prominent
289ca1de.description: The Voigtländer Prominent is a 35mm rangefinder camera made by Voigtlander. It features proprietary bayonet lens mount, Synchro-Compur leaf, behind-lens shutter.

dfcd43df.name: Voigtlander Voigtländer Vitessa
dfcd43df.description: The Voigtländer Vitessa is a 35mm rangefinder camera made by Voigtlander. It features fixed or DKL-mount (Vitessa T) lens mount, Synchro-Compur leaf shutter.

9aa5742c.name: Voigtlander Voigtländer Vito
9aa5742c.description: The Voigtländer Vito is a 35mm rangefinder camera made by Voigtlander. It features fixed lens mount, Compur and Prontor leaf shutter.

d8cd8712.name: Voigtlander Voigtländer Bessamatic and Ultramatic
d8cd8712.description: The Voigtländer Bessamatic and Ultramatic is a 35mm SLR camera made by Voigtlander. It features DKL-mount lens mount, Synchro-Compur leaf shutter.

eac1cd24.name: Voigtlander Voigtländer Brillant
eac1cd24.description: The Voigtländer Brillant is a medium format (120) Medium format made by Voigtlander.

33cf5d22.name: Edixa Reflex
33cf5d22.description: The Edixa Reflex cameras, introduced in 1954, were West Germany's most popular own series of SLR's with focal plane shutter. The original name of the first Edixa SLR was Komet.

f1e3e6c2.name: ADOX Polo
f1e3e6c2.description: The ADOX Polo was a consumer-class, viewfinder camera with a completely manual operating system. It was manufactured between 1960 and 1963.

3295986f.name: Ducati Sogno
3295986f.description: The Ducati Sogno ("dream") was a half-frame 35 mm rangefinder camera made by Ducati in the 1950s at its Milan factory. The Sogno has been called a "miniature Leica" referring to its size and build quality; it is considerably smaller than a Leica III.

89b629da.name: FED 2
89b629da.description: The FED 2 was a 35 mm rangefinder camera introduced in 1955 by FED. The name of FED comes from the initial of Felix Edmundovich Dzerzhinsky.

192e4377.name: Foca camera
192e4377.description: The Foca camera was a French-built brand of rangefinder cameras made by Optique & Précision de Levallois. The camera was designed in 1938, but the Second World War prevented its release, which finally occurred in 1945.

ca51723d.name: Zorki 1
ca51723d.description: The Zorki 1 is a 35mm rangefinder camera made by Zorki.

affcb591.name: Zorki 4
affcb591.description: The Zorki 4 is a 35mm rangefinder camera made by Zorki. It features 39LTM lens mount.

df1f712f.name: Diana (camera)
df1f712f.description: The Diana camera is a plastic-bodied camera that originally used 120 roll film. Contemporary variants use 35mm film or 110 format, and digital versions are also available.

11108140.name: Various Holga
11108140.description: The Holga is a medium format 120 film camera, made in Hong Kong, known for its low-fidelity aesthetic. The Holga's low-cost construction and simple meniscus lens often yields pictures that display vignetting, blur, light leaks and other distortions.

323b3534.name: Horizon (camera)
323b3534.description: The Horizon (Russian: Горизонт) is a mechanical swing-lens panoramic camera. It is manufactured by Krasnogorsky Mechanicheskiy Zavod (KMZ) in Krasnogorsk, Russia, better-known for their range of Zenit cameras.

1b7dad4d.name: Zenit Krasnogorsk-3
1b7dad4d.description: The Krasnogorsk-3 (Красногорск-3) is a spring-wound 16mm mirror-reflex movie camera designed and manufactured in the Soviet Union by Krasnogorsk Mechanical Works. A total of 105,435 Krasnogorsk-3 cameras were produced between 1971 and 1993.

specs.lens: M42 lens mount or Russian bayonette mount

2f3aaf68.name: Sport (camera)
2f3aaf68.description: The Sport (camera) is a 35mm SLR camera made by Sport. It features bayonet lens mount.

d7b81e6d.name: Zenit Start (Soviet camera)
d7b81e6d.description: The Start (Soviet camera) is a 35mm SLR camera produced by Zenit from 1958 to 1964. It features Proprietary (breech-locking bayonet) lens mount, B, 500, 200, 100, 50, 25 shutter. It weighs 300 g.

9dbd20a6.name: Feinwerktechnik Gmbh Mec 16 SB
9dbd20a6.description: The MEC 16 SB is a German 16 mm subminiature camera launched in 1957 by Feinwerktechnik GmbH of Lahr, Germany. The lens is a Rodenstock Heligon 1:2/22mm with diaphragm working on principle of "cat's pupil".

dcddd475.name: Sawyer's, View-Master Personal Stereo Camera
dcddd475.description: The View-Master Personal Stereo Camera is a 35mm stereo camera made by Sawyer's,. It features 10–100 and Bulb (continuously adjustable) shutter.

52754b66.name: Panon Camera Shoko Widelux
52754b66.description: A Swing-lens Panoramic camera by Panon Camera Shoko.

f80dd872.name: Calypso (camera)
f80dd872.description: The self-contained amphibious underwater Calypso 35mm film camera was conceived by the marine explorer Jacques Cousteau (1910–1997), designed by Jean de Wouters and manufactured by Atoms in France.

b72340ae.name: Canon EOS IX
b72340ae.description: The EOS IX (world markets) or EOS IX E is an APS-format single-lens reflex camera that was introduced by Canon Inc. of Japan in October 1996 as part of their EOS series SLR cameras.

9a3d9d66.name: Canon T60
9a3d9d66.description: The Canon T60 was the last manual focus FD-mount 35 mm single-lens reflex (SLR) camera sold by Canon; it was introduced in 1990, three years after the introduction of Canon's incompatible EOS system of autofocus SLRs and their EF lenses. It was the final camera in Canon's T series. It was introduced solely as a cheap SLR system for export.

e439a794.name: Canon T70
e439a794.description: The Canon T70 was a 35mm FD-mount single-lens reflex camera introduced in April 1984 as the second in Canon's T series. The T70 started with the concepts explored in 1983's T50, took them further, and applied them to a more sophisticated camera.

0a941119.name: Canon T90
0a941119.description: The Canon T90, introduced in 1986, was the top of the line in Canon's T series of 35 mm Single-lens reflex (SLR) cameras. It is the last professional-level manual-focus camera from Canon, and the last professional camera to use the Canon FD lens mount.

13fc9be8.name: Canon T90 Transparent

4a35cf8a.name: Canon TLb
4a35cf8a.description: The Canon TLb is a 35 mm single-lens reflex camera introduced by Canon in September 1974. It features a Canon FD lens mount, and is also compatible with Canon's earlier FL-mount lenses in stop-down metering mode. The TLb was a cheaper version of the Canon FTb for the export market, as was the slightly later TX.
